flow_name: Univest – Variant 4: Crown + Trust Pillars + ‘Unlock FREE trade’

sample_quality.note: At n=50, completion rate has ±11.5% uncertainty at 90% confidence. Adequate for directional decisions; run 100+ for statistically robust sub-segment comparisons.

top_friction_points.friction: crown branding feels overly grandiose — 'trying too hard' to signal premium quality
trust pillars are helpful but their layout competes with the CTA for visual attention
accuracy claim still without methodology — trust pillars partially offset it but don't eliminate concern
unlock framing still unclear about what specifically is being unlocked and at what cost

executive_summary: Univest – Variant 4: Crown + Trust Pillars + ‘Unlock FREE trade’ achieved a 44.0% completion rate across all 50 personas (mid-tier of the six variants tested). The single-screen format reduced decision friction significantly. Core non-converters are Skeptics (0.0% completion), Privacy Guardians (0.0%), and the Junk cohort (0%). Enthusiasts convert at 93.3% across all variants.

usability_findings.finding: The 84.7% accuracy claim is the most-cited friction point across all archetypes. Without a methodology link it reads as an invented marketing number.
usability_findings.evidence: Why is it exactly 84.7%? That specificity without evidence is a classic sign of fabricated social proof.
usability_findings.recommendation: Replace with a 'Verified 3-year performance summary' with a link to historical call data.

segment_analysis.summary: Enthusiasts lead conversion (93.3%). Skeptics and Privacy Guardians anchor the bottom (0.0%). Pragmatists are the swing segment — most sensitive to trust-signal quality and pricing transparency.
segment_analysis.high_propensity_segment: The Enthusiast (Male/Female, 23-31, ₹22k-₹75k). Low friction threshold, impulsive ₹1 tap, FOMO-driven.
segment_analysis.low_propensity_segment: The Skeptic and Privacy Guardian (Mixed, 28-41). Systematic trust evaluation, dark-pattern detection, household financial accountability.

4.reasoning: My professional/analytical framework flagged Variant 4: unverified accuracy claims + dark-pattern pricing is an automatic reject.

fix_recommendations.root_cause: 84.7% accuracy claim perceived as fin-influencer bait with no methodology
fix_recommendations.recommendation: Replace with a verified 3-year win-rate range and link to methodology

fix_recommendations.root_cause: SEBI registration and trust signals below the fold on smaller screens
fix_recommendations.recommendation: Pin SEBI reg number and user count in the top 200px viewport across all device sizes

flow_assessment.overall_verdict: Crown branding polarises: authority signal for some, tryhard for others. Trust pillars are the strongest trust-building element but the layout competes for visual hierarchy.

emotional_journey_map.completers: Crown + SEBI + user count → authority established → tap
emotional_journey_map.drop_offs: Crown feels gimmicky → scrutinise trust claims → find accuracy claim unverified → exit

persona_details.professional_background: NIT Surat grad, 8 yrs at Mumbai fintech startups. Trades Bank Nifty options 2-3x/week on Zerodha and Dhan. Burned by Telegram tip groups in 2022 but distinguishes those from SEBI-registered platforms.

persona_details.overall_monologue: Rohit ran the Crown/Trust Pillars variant through a fast mental checklist: SEBI visible, ₹1 is research-level cost, trial is cancellable. Completed with residual concern about the accuracy claim but curiosity won out. Will evaluate the actual signal quality before judging the product.

screen_monologues.internal_monologue: The screen with the crown logo and trust pillars: sebi registration, 75+ years combined experience, 5 lakh+ users ticked enough boxes. Not perfect, but ₹1 gets me past the trust paywall to see the actual product. I can evaluate properly once I'm inside.
